reviews.review_msg: If you really want to learn then Prof. Reilly is one of the best professors I have ever had.  Challenging and fulfilling.  Loves it.

Don't take a class with her. I took a lower division course and it was fine, but once you hit upper division she turns into a savage with the smaller class size. Seems nice on the surface but will absolutely rip you to shreds if you misunderstand something. Very tough grader. Super condescending.

Avoid her at all costs, your GPA will go down and therefore it is NOT worth taking her classes, even if the syllabus seems easy. She is very two faced and acts nice in class but then turns into a savage when marking/emailing.

Katherine is a really great prof. There's so much to learn from her and she is truly awesome and caring. Wish there was more assignments to even out the grade weight so assignments aren't worth so much.

No clear grading criteria which makes it difficult to be successful. Doesnt encourage creativity. Super condescending and rude. Thinks shes always right and refuses to accept any other opinions. Would absolutely not recommend this prof.
With this proff, if she doesn't like/agrees with your ideas, topic, or question, you will not get a productive answer or anything more than a belittling reply.

There are so many amazing upper division CMNS courses, unfortunately this is not one of them. Questions are not usually welcome, and assignments are incredibly unclear. prof may appear friendly at first, but is the opposite. the general upset & confusion from the class becomes more obvious every week into the semester.

I wish her icon wasn't green because then I would have known not to take this class. Quite condescending, approximately 120 pages of reading a week, doesn't give clear outlines of her grading criteria and expects you to read her mind of else get a bad grade. Has a different student do the lecture every week, no idea how I can learn this way.

the course itself is still developping so its a bit unorganized. hard to get a good idea of what they wanted in assignments. Shesa good lecturer, i enjoyed listening. But dont go to her with questions. Shes a bit rude in-person. Maybe just not a "people"person. She even emailed us To ask qs to the tas not her. good as long as u dont need help.

While the course material and lectures were clear, the assignments were not. This was apparently a new way of teaching this course and it showed. Two of the assignments required a program that was never taught in classes or tutorials. TA was unhelpful. Assignment guidelines were confusing and unclear of what you would be graded for/on.

The lectures are very important to attend because all of the assignments are tied to the lectures. Professor Reilly is very friendly and always welcome to help with the problems. However,  my TA wasn't very helpful, he would talk most of the time and give confusing examples. Back to prof, explains materials very clearly, caring, and very open.

Professor Reilly is an outstanding lecturer. You will go from taking this class as mandatory credit and to leaving inspired. Go to lecture. Her lessons and teachings are invaluable. However, the quizzes and midterms were positivist beasts: convoluted and ambiguous multiple choice or fill in the blank questions. It won't matter how long you study.

She summarizes the readings for you with her lectures. Her lecture notes are very helpful. She is easy to understand, and clear. Super nice, super fun. She made dry content interesting and not dry. Hands down best prof I've ever had!
VERY condescending and rude. She makes you feel like an idiot. She seems nice at first but that doesn't last long. Avoid her at all costs.
Great prof, dresses real nice.  I'm in love.

I enjoyed this course a lot.  While the material can be a little dry, Prof. Reilly teaches the fundamentals needed in Design and Method in Qualitative Communication Research to success in field research.  With a title like that, I didn't expect this to be a creative writing course.

The course was generally easy with help from a good TA, but Reilly really makes my blood boil.She is very belittling and makes you feel stupid when answering any questions you might have.She comes off as a sweet, gentle lady for one second, then the next she is a completely condescending, impatient shrew.

I have no idea why these previous reviews are the way they are. Cmns 262 was absolutely fine. She's very clear when she lectures and she IS teaching about positivist theory since it is a methods class. If you don't like the material, then don't take the class, it's not her fault. Anyways, she's great, but the mark really depends on your TA.
extremely boring and dry.
She is a nice prof, but seems to expect much from the students,dont take cmns262 with her,super boring materials,I felt like liking a philosophy class in the first half of the course.
This class did not have a good flow to it and the material was incredibly boring. Tutorials were a snoozer.

Prof. Reilly is really passionate about what she teaches. This particular course was fairly self-directed in a lot of ways, but she offered great feedback and lots of extra help to anyone who needed it.

Katherine is an AWESOME prof! She is very passionate in the material she teaches and has a way of explaining it to her students that makes difficult concepts appear crystal clear. She is super helpful in office hours and cares a great deal about her students. I would highly recommend her; she has been one of my favourite profs in four years at SFU!
Very dry content.
